sourceMovie.title: V.I.P.
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit V.I.P. for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Park Hoon-jung keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Once Upon a Time in Mexico
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If V.I.P. clicked for you, Once Upon a Time in Mexico (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after V.I.P.?
faq.answer: Try Once Upon a Time in Mexico, Spy Game, Street Kings—each shares DNA with V.I.P. while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is V.I.P.?
faq.answer: V.I.P. (2017) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is V.I.P. worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
